channel.title: Avery Hill
channel.description: Barber is a blog theme for Jekyll built by Thomas Vaeth for Samesies using HTML, Sass, and JavaScript.

item.title: Large Hiking Dress
item.description: I wanted to make a dress for a friend that is too tall and broad-shouldered for most dresses– furthermore, I wanted the material to be good for hiking: light, quickdrying, and breathable. Apparently taslanated nylon is a material with these properties, commonly used to make various outdoor clothing. I turned a sewing guide for a simple A-line dress into an R script that builds a dress pattern from 3 simple body measurements (please ask me for the code until I get a github repository setup). After inputing height, bust, and shoulder measurements, the program produces something like this: That can then be mapped onto parchment paper and cut out to form a pattern: After cutting out the material from the patterns, I sewed the top of the shoulders, and down the sides of the dress, and added bias tape to the neckline and arm holes. I finished by hemming the bottom to the desired height.

item.title: Plant Species Flashcards
item.description:
This is a javascript flashcard program that helps a person learn plant species in a particular geographic area. It uses an R script to build the image directories and csv files necessary for the flashcard program to work. The flashcards are grouped by 10 plants so that a user can learn a few plants before moving on to the next group.

The front of each card looks like:

And back:

If you’re interested in messing around with incomplete but functional(?) version you can do so here

All images are stitched together from CalFlora.

item.title: Kinetic Sculpture Race
item.description:
This was one of my first major projects. I entered in a competition to build a human-powered vehicle that could traverse land, sand, and water for my high school senior project. The Kinetic Sculpture Race Grand Championship has been an annual tradition in Arcata, CA since 1969, in which dozens of DIY engineers build vehicles and race eachother over 3 days and 42 miles in Humboldt County.

It started off with some bikes:

And a plywood frame:

To make a ~ 400 lbs vehicle with very sketchy brakes and an old tractor seat:

A friend helped the with the art, and I attached ropes to control the steering:

Completing the 42 miles was a surprise to myself and everyone that helped me:
